_comment: Expansion candidates mined from data/rmit_vn_2026-06-17.json (the fetched RMIT VN catalogue), scored through the same rubric as examples/rmit_vn_scores.example.json. The incumbent #247 PEAD/Sentiment is included as the benchmark. QR-career lens. See docs/expanded_projects.md. Run: python evaluate.py examples/expanded_projects_scores.json --sensitivity

projects.project: #247 PEAD/Sentiment — Investor Sentiment, Earnings Surprises & Market Anomalies (incumbent benchmark)

significance.rationale: PEAD and sentiment-driven anomalies are open, actively-studied questions in empirical finance.

originality.rationale: Sentiment x earnings-surprise framing on a less-studied market is a defensible gap.

rigour.rationale: Anomaly prediction has a falsifiable metric: out-of-sample, risk-adjusted returns.

feasibility.rationale: Prices, earnings and sentiment data are obtainable; scope fits a candidature.

resourcing.rationale: Funded; three named supervisors across Accounting AISSC.

impact.rationale: Publishable empirical-finance results that map directly to alpha claims.

candidate_fit.rationale: Anomaly prediction is alpha — squarely on the QR career path and existing skills.

ethics.rationale: Secondary market data; no human-subjects concern.

availability.rationale: Open until 31 Dec 2027; not yet contacted.

projects.project: #238 Social finance as a predictor of firm outcomes & asset prices (ASEAN) — Linh Nguyen Thi My, DR203

significance.rationale: Social-finance asset pricing (Kuchler & Stroebel) is a hot, open area; emerging-market evidence is scarce.

originality.rationale: Social interactions -> asset prices in an ASEAN/Vietnam setting is a genuine, defensible gap.

rigour.rationale: Asset prices in the brief + Financial Econometrics FOR code => native falsifiable OOS return metric.

feasibility.rationale: Measuring social-interaction proxies is data-hard; ASEAN-wide scope is broad and needs narrowing.

resourcing.rationale: Finance supervisor with econometrics mandate, but a single named lead and listing reads expired -> capacity must be confirmed.

impact.rationale: Asset-price results publish in finance venues and map to alpha; ESG-free supervisory line keeps the framing clean.

candidate_fit.rationale: Social/sentiment -> asset prices is the PEAD core under a cleaner, more alpha-shaped supervisor than #247.

ethics.rationale: Secondary market/social data; no human-subjects concern.

availability.rationale: Advertised close 31/12/23 — reads as expired. Gate fails until Linh Nguyen Thi My confirms a current intake.

projects.project: #167 FinTech and Sustainable Finance — Huy Pham, Hanh Le, Van Ha, DR205 (funded, open 2026-12-31)

significance.rationale: FinTech x sustainable finance is consequential and policy-relevant.

originality.rationale: Crowded ESG-fintech / green-asset space; incremental novelty.

rigour.rationale: ESG-impact assessment is looser to falsify; an econometrics component exists but is not the core.

feasibility.rationale: Funded and open; ESG/market data accessible; scope fits a candidature.

resourcing.rationale: Funded, open to 2026-12-31, three named supervisors with a Financial Econometrics FOR share.

impact.rationale: Publishable and policy-visible, with real industry interest.

candidate_fit.rationale: ESG/green/blockchain framing pulls toward disclosure-impact, off the alpha path (same divergence risk as #247's ESG lead).

ethics.rationale: Market/ESG secondary data; standard integrity controls.

projects.project: #177 Sustainable Finance and Market Stability: green investments — Fiza Qureshi, DR205

significance.rationale: Green-asset market dynamics matter but the question is broad and developing.

originality.rationale: A new application of established market-dynamics methods to green assets; modest novelty.

rigour.rationale: Explicitly quantitative, but 'market stability' is loosely defined as a success metric.

feasibility.rationale: Quantitative on issuable-securities data, but very broad (developed + emerging) and needs scoping.

resourcing.rationale: Single supervisor; funding and close date not listed — status uncertain.

impact.rationale: Publishable in mid-tier sustainable-finance venues; limited alpha reach.

candidate_fit.rationale: ESG/green-investment focus; Investment & Risk-Management FOR code is the only real QR hook.

availability.rationale: Listed with no close date; assume open pending confirmation.

projects.project: #205 What drives the development of Cryptoeconomies? — Binh Nguyen Thanh, Diem Vo, DR205

significance.rationale: Crypto/digital-asset growth matters, but framed as development economics, not pricing.

originality.rationale: Theory-building for cryptoeconomy development; literature is early but not asset-pricing-novel.

rigour.rationale: Theory-driven/development framing; no falsifiable return claim.

feasibility.rationale: Doable but conceptual scope is diffuse.

resourcing.rationale: Unfunded; two supervisors but no funding signalled.

impact.rationale: Publishable in fintech/development outlets; limited alpha reach.

candidate_fit.rationale: Development-economics-of-crypto, not crypto return prediction — off the alpha path.

availability.rationale: Listed with no close date; assume open.

projects.project: #221 Blockchain, FinTech and transformation in banking — Thai Nguyen, DR205 (funded, open 2027)

significance.rationale: Banking disruption by fintech matters but is a busy, well-trodden area.

originality.rationale: Industry-impact and policy questions; little identifiable new method or dataset.

rigour.rationale: Qualitative impact/policy framing; hard to pin a falsifiable success metric.

feasibility.rationale: Funded and open to 2027; scope achievable.

resourcing.rationale: Funded, open, named supervisor (offshore enrolment via RMIT Australia).

impact.rationale: Publishable in banking/policy venues; negligible alpha reach.

candidate_fit.rationale: Qualitative banking-transformation work, off the QR/alpha path.
